attempts.strategy: Two-step Mathlib chain bridging ExactAt n on the Čech cochain complex to Subsingleton on the cohomology carrier. h.isZero_homology lands IsZero of the homology; ModuleCat.subsingleton_of_isZero upgrades IsZero on ModuleCat R to Subsingleton on its carrier type.
attempts.goal_before: Subsingleton (cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n) with h : (cechCochain C F 𝒰).ExactAt n in context
attempts.insight: Structural reduction from ExactAt n to Subsingleton on the cohomology carrier is universal — does not depend on the cover, the sheaf, or any project-specific machinery. Reusable across every future IsCechAcyclicCover instance proof.

next_steps: Helper is closed kernel-only. No further action required; it serves as the structural skeleton for every IsCechAcyclicCover instance proof in the project.

findings.blocker: Single transient sorry at L1729 covers substeps (a)+(b)+(c): (a) extra-degeneracy at D(f) via FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech + ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv + op-passage; (b) localised-Čech identification via iter-057/058/059 chained with IsLocalizedModule.map_exact; (c) local-to-global via exact_of_isLocalized_span. Full assembly estimated at ~150-250 LOC across 2-3 prover sessions (per the analogy report).

The remaining single sorry is substantive (covers an actual computational step — the augmented-Čech extra-degeneracy + local-to-global lift), not a thin glue lemma. Achieves the directive's 'Acceptable' success mode trending toward 'Good'.

attempts.insight: Rejected by directive: 'Closing … by assuming s = ∅, …, or any other shortcut that bypasses the localization-then-extra-degeneracy argument' is explicitly listed as a forbidden dead end in PROGRESS.md. Vacuous closure bypasses the substantive cohomological content.

attempts.strategy: Close via n = 1 special case + Mayer-Vietoris induction (n = 1: sheaf-axiom Čech condition; n > 1: Mayer-Vietoris reduction through HasCechToHModuleIso)
attempts.goal_before: (cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)).ExactAt n with hn : 0 < n
attempts.insight: Rejected: Mayer-Vietoris reduction routes through HasCechToHModuleIso (Branch B of the analogy report) — directive said NOT to attempt in the same iteration as IsCechAcyclicCover. Also n = 1 needs the sheaf-axiom for toModuleKSheaf on basic-open covers, which is non-trivial setup.

next_steps: Iter-061 should attempt substep (b) — the localised-Čech identification — first, since it's a definition + identification iso (not a theorem). With (b) in scope, substep (a) becomes a Mathlib-chain repackaging (~30-40 LOC) and substep (c) becomes a 1-2 line exact_of_isLocalized_span application. Per the no-pre-proving policy, substep (b)'s definition should be folded into the iter-061 prover session, not pre-extracted as a standalone iter-061 objective. Estimated cohort: 2-3 prover sessions, ~150-250 LOC total.
